name: Pepper-&-Tomato Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons Olive or sunflower seed oil
4 Garlic cloves, peeled and coarsely chopped
1 pound Red peppers or pimientos, thinly sliced
1 pound Very ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped (juice reserved)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove stems, seeds, and veins from chiles. Tear the flesh into a few large pieces, cover with 1 cup water in a small saucepan, and bring to boil.
2. Simmer 20 minutes, then puree in a blender.
3. Heat oil in a pan and add onions, bay leaves, cloves, coriander, parsley, and garlic. Cook over medium heat for several minutes until onions have begun to soften.
4. Add salt, peppers, and cabbage. Stir to combine and coat vegetables with oil.
5. Add 1/2 cup water or stock, cover the pan, and cook over low heat for 10 minutes.
6. When the vegetables have wilted, stir in 1/4 cup pureed chiles or 1 to 2 teaspoons chili powder.
7. Add tomatoes, their juice, and remaining water or stock. Bring to boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer very slowly for 30 minutes.
8. Remove bay leaves and puree the soup until completely blended. Return it to the pan and season with salt if needed. Add more chile if desired.
9. Serve soup with a sprinkling of chopped cilantro.
